name: United States (vice-presidential standard)

colors.symbolism: the eagle's chief and the four stars of office

description: A white field bearing in the centre the Seal of the Vice President of the United States: a bald eagle with outstretched wings clasping an olive branch and a bundle of arrows, with a striped shield on its breast and a scroll reading E PLURIBUS UNUM, surrounded by a ring of stars and a circular band; a five-pointed dark-blue star is set in each of the four corners.
history: The modern vice-presidential flag was redesigned in 1975 under President Gerald Ford to align stylistically with the presidential standard. A dark-blue star in each corner distinguishes it from the president's flag.

features.role: Seal of the Vice President of the United States
features.symbolism: an eagle clutching olive branch and arrows, with the shield of the United States on its breast

features.role: one star in each corner of the flag
